conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some information.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex, he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand a bit more about your situation.

Does Alex normally live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Next, I need to ask:

Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're responsible for things like providing for his food, clothing, and overall care.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of that - his food, clothing, and everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside in the UK (for example, you're a UK citizen, have settled status, or have another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester - and I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Is Alex under 16 years old, or if he's 16 or over, is he in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's only 8, so yes, he's under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One more important question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for answering all my questions. Before I provide my final assessment, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official guidance, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

You meet all the key requirements:
- Alex is under 16 (he's 8 years old)
- He lives with you
- You're responsible for his care, food, clothing, and everything he needs
- You live in the UK with the right to reside here
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for Alex. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if: (1) you're responsible for a child under 16, and (2) you live in the UK. Alex meets these criteria because: he is 8 years old (under 16); he lives with you; you are responsible for his care including food, clothing, and all his needs; you live in the UK (Manchester) and have the right to reside here; and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for him. All the eligibility requirements are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. The claimant meets all the key requirements: they are responsible for Alex who is under 16, Alex lives with them, they reside in the UK with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
